context.street_character: One-way WB | 30 km/h | Sub-standard sidewalks & roadway | E-W commercial connector

assessment.signage: Scent of Morocco (upper banner); The Revue Bar (marquee sign x2); 195 New Seaway Fish Market (adjacent)

facade_detail.composition: The street frontage spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys beneath a cross-gable, consistent with its commercial, row typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade is organized as a storefront base with 2 upper-window bays, using double-hung sash windows, and 1 principal entrance.

facade_description: Two-storey brick facade with a cross-gable roof, approximately 5.2 m wide, built flush to the sidewalk. The ground floor is an active commercial storefront (Vida Market) beneath two double-hung sash windows at the second floor. The facade is in poor condition with damaged masonry noted. Signage for Scent of Morocco and The Revue Bar is visible on the upper portion. Part of a commercial row dating to 1914-1930. No direct photo observation is available; description is based on database and HCD records.
